name: Kemba Charleston
description_short: Kemba Charleston Federal Credit Union is a West Virginia-based member-owned credit union founded in 1936, offering checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and financial services to over 4,600 members with $40M+ in assets.
description_long:
Kemba Charleston Federal Credit Union was organized in 1935 and received its Federal Charter Organization Certificate on January 16, 1936, making it the first federally-chartered credit union in West Virginia. Over nearly nine decades, the institution has grown to serve more than 4,600 members and maintains over $40,000,000 in assets. As a member-owned, not-for-profit cooperative chartered by the federal government, Kemba Charleston operates under NCUA insurance protection, distinguishing it from for-profit banks.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products spanning deposit accounts, lending, and specialized services. On the deposit side, members can access checking accounts with debit/ATM cards, multiple savings vehicles (regular shares, premium savings, share certificates, Kids' Club, Christmas Club), and IRA accounts. For borrowing, Kemba Charleston provides vehicle loans (auto, ATV, motorcycle, RV with GAP insurance), personal loans (signature loans, lines of credit, share-secured loans, MasterCard), special purpose loans, and mortgage services including purchase, refinance, home equity, and second mortgages. Digital services include KembaConnection online banking, mobile banking, online bill pay, e-statements, and a loan application center.

Kemba Charleston distinguishes itself through its deep roots in West Virginia history as the state's first federally-chartered credit union and its emphasis on member financial counseling and community involvement. The institution provides travel-related services, insurance products through TruStage, and convenience tools like ATM locators and car-buying calculators. Their membership eligibility includes select employee groups, and they actively engage in community sponsorships and events. The credit union completed a significant online banking system upgrade in October 2023 and maintains multiple contact channels including phone, email, and a member voice feedback system.

Kemba Charleston Federal Credit Union is best suited for West Virginia residents and eligible employee groups seeking relationship-based banking with competitive rates and personalized service. The primary limitation is geographic and membership eligibility constraints—membership is not universally available and is restricted to specific employee groups and communities served. As a smaller regional credit union with $40M in assets, they may have fewer digital features or branch locations compared to national banks, though their community focus and member-owned structure provide advantages in pricing and personalized financial counseling.
services: Checking accounts with debit card and ATM access
Multiple savings accounts (regular shares, premium savings, share certificates, Kids' Club, Christmas Club, Little Star Savers Club)
Vehicle loans (auto, ATV, motorcycle, RV) with optional GAP insurance
Personal loans (signature loans, lines of credit, share-secured loans)
Mortgage services (purchase, refinance, home equity, 2nd mortgage)
Online banking platform (KembaConnection) with login ID and security code authentication
pros: First federally-chartered credit union in West Virginia with nearly 90 years of operational history and stability
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure typically results in lower fees and more competitive loan/savings rates than for-profit banks
NCUA insurance protection on deposits up to $250,000
Comprehensive loan offerings including vehicle loans with GAP insurance, mortgages with multiple options (purchase, refinance, home equity, 2nd mortgage), and personal loans
Free financial counseling services available to members
Modern digital banking platform with KembaConnection online/mobile banking, bill pay, and e-statements
Active community involvement with sponsorships, events, and member engagement programs
Multiple contact options including 24/7 phone support (1-800-800-5322) and ATM locator service
cons: Membership eligibility is restricted to select employee groups and geographic areas—not open to all consumers
Limited to West Virginia service area with presumably fewer physical branch locations compared to national banks
Smaller asset base ($40M) may limit loan amounts and specialty products compared to regional or national credit unions
Website does not clearly specify current interest rates, fee schedules, or loan terms, requiring members to contact directly or log in
No mention of specialty services like student loans, business loans, or alternative products that larger credit unions offer
best_for: West Virginia residents and employees of select groups eligible for membership seeking relationship-based banking
Members prioritizing community-focused financial institutions with competitive rates and personalized service
Consumers applying for mortgages, vehicle loans, or personal loans who benefit from credit union lending practices
Savers interested in IRA and share certificate products with member-owner pricing advantages
diagnosis: Kemba Charleston Federal Credit Union is best for West Virginia residents and employees of select eligible groups seeking membership-based banking with competitive rates, personalized service, and a community focus. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted—this is not a bank open to the general public, and eligibility depends on employment affiliation or geographic service area qualification.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Kemba Charleston — Credit Union in Dunbar, WV | CreditDoc
meta_description: Kemba Charleston — NCUA-insured credit union in Dunbar, WV. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
